depends_on.name: element_id
depends_on.documentation: The document ID of the element this image is associated with. Optional, since some image_zarr documents (e.g., raw acquisitions not yet bound to an experimental element) may not have an element at write time.

fields.name: omero_channels
fields.documentation: Per-channel rendering metadata in OMERO/OME-Zarr style. One element per imaging channel, ordered to match the channel axis of the native array. May be empty when the writer did not record OMERO metadata (channel labels/colors are then unknown). Most writers populate at least 'label' and 'active'; 'color', 'family', 'coefficient', and 'window' are commonly absent.

fields.name: label
fields.documentation: Human-readable channel name (e.g., 'GFP', 'DAPI', 'ch0', 'ch1'). Free-form string; writers conventionally use short identifiers.

fields.name: color
fields.documentation: RGB rendering color as a six-character hex string without leading '#' (e.g., 'FF0000' for red, '00FF00' for green). Empty when the writer did not record a color.

fields.name: active
fields.documentation: Whether this channel is shown by default in OMERO/napari rendering. True if absent in the source writer.

fields.name: family
fields.documentation: Color-map family (e.g., 'linear', 'log', 'polynomial'). Empty when the writer did not record one.

fields.name: coefficient
fields.documentation: Family-specific shaping parameter (typically gamma for 'linear' / 'polynomial'). Null when not recorded.

fields.name: window
fields.documentation: Display window for this channel. 'min' and 'max' are the full data range; 'start' and 'end' are the initial display range (mapped to the rendered color ramp's endpoints). Null when not recorded.

fields.name: min
fields.documentation: Full data range minimum for this channel.

fields.name: max
fields.documentation: Full data range maximum for this channel.

fields.name: start
fields.documentation: Initial display-window minimum for this channel.

fields.name: end
fields.documentation: Initial display-window maximum for this channel.

fields.name: num_channels
fields.documentation: Number of imaging channels. Denormalised for cheap querying ('find all multi-channel images'). Derivable from the native shape and the position of the channel axis; 0 when there is no channel axis. Consumer tooling populates this at ingest from the discovered channel axis.

fields.name: num_timepoints
fields.documentation: Number of timepoints in the native array. Denormalised for cheap querying ('find time-lapse images with more than N frames'). 0 when there is no time axis (e.g., a single 3D volume). Consumer tooling populates this at ingest from the discovered time axis.

fields.name: pixel_size_x
fields.documentation: Physical pixel size along the x axis at the native level, as a length composite (canonical: meters). Denormalised from multiscales[0].datasets[0].scale and axes[*].unit so the typical query 'find images with sub-micrometer x resolution' is one operator. Null when there is no x axis or when the writer did not record a scale.

fields.name: pixel_size_y
fields.documentation: Physical pixel size along the y axis at the native level. Same conventions as pixel_size_x.

fields.name: pixel_size_z
fields.documentation: Physical pixel size along the z axis at the native level. Null for 2D images. Same conventions as pixel_size_x.
